rationale: The response immediately triggers the brake, names the proposed sorting-table confirmer as a fourth downstream manual patch, and shifts the diagnosis upstream to recurring identification, handoff, and system-state validation failure. It does allow a tightly bounded one-batch emergency confirmer, but explicitly refuses making it the default or a standing gate and requires root-cause records plus a structural repair owner and deadline. That satisfies the anti-spiral triage rather than directly adding another manual checkpoint.
